arts.ttl: Team News: Three changes, two debuts for Palace
arts.sum: Maxence Lacroix and Eddie Nketiah will make their Crystal Palace debuts against Leicester City on Saturday afternoon, with Cheick Doucouré also back in the starting XI.

arts.ttl: Live Blog: Crystal Palace v Leicester City
arts.sum: Crystal Palace return to Premier League action against Leicester City at Selhurst Park this afternoon (14th September, 15:00 BST) – follow live updates from SE25 below!

arts.ttl: Leicester City's 25-man squad published with one space left empty
arts.sum: Leicester City have one spare space in their Premier League squad with just 24 players named on their official list for the first half of the season. City did have two players in their development squad who would have needed to be registered to feature in the Premier League this season, but neither have been handed a spot.

arts.ttl: Eddie Nketiah told he has point to prove after surprise Arsenal exit
arts.sum: The striker swapped the Emirates for Selhurst Park and could make his Crystal Palace debut when they take on Leicester City

arts.ttl: FPL Gameweek 4 predicted line-ups: Crystal Palace v Leicester
arts.sum: Team form, absentees and our predicted XIs for Crystal Palace and Leicester The post FPL Gameweek 4 predicted line-ups: Crystal Palace v Leicester appeared first on Best FPL Tips, Advice, Team News, Picks, and Statistics from Fantasy Football Scout.

arts.ttl: Glasner praises Academy setup and outlines pathway for progress
arts.sum: Speaking ahead of the clash against Leicester City on Saturday, Oliver Glasner was full of praise for Palace’s Academy prospects and the pathway the club has to offer them.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace boss quizzed on strength of squad after transfer window - and makes future formation admission
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner says his current crop of Crystal Palace players have to prove they are better than last season – and is open to the idea of changing formation with the players he has at his disposal. Glasner said: “We have to prove it – it’s maybe a little bit different.

arts.ttl: Leicester City predicted line-up v Crystal Palace as Steve Cooper faces three big decisions
arts.sum: Facundo Buonanotte’s potential return to the Leicester City starting line-up is at the heart of one of the big decisions Steve Cooper faces as he picks his team to take on Crystal Palace. Given City were a much greater threat against Villa after his introduction, Buonanotte will be the favourite to start in attacking midfield on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Bilal El Khannouss selection admission made ahead of Leicester City trip to Crystal Palace
arts.sum: Bilal El Khannouss could add spontaneity to Leicester City’s attack – but only because he’s trained so little with his new club. “But we’re in a situation now today where he’s trained twice with us.

arts.ttl: Palace look for first league win as they welcome newly promoted Leicester City
arts.sum: Things finally feel as if they may be starting to click and the visit of Leicester City, back in the top-flight after a year's absence, looks a good opportunity for Palace to grab their first league win of the season. Palace’s spirited second-half performance away at Chelsea provided the club with a timely confidence boost ahead of the first international break of the season.
